commit.message:
fix(guardian): correct Batch J review regressions

Summary:
- treat current_event as a live observer anchor in the competing-project risk gate
- apply live scope tie tolerance before choosing the winning scoped learning signal
- add regressions for current-event anchoring and near-tie scope selection
- record the PR review follow-up in the guardian implementation log

Rationale:
- Codex review on PR #254 found two real logic gaps in Batch J policy behavior
- the world model could incorrectly downgrade confidence during live current-event focus
- scoped live learning could report a tie reason without actually changing the selected scope

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/guardian/world_model.py backend/src/guardian/feedback.py backend/tests/test_guardian_feedback.py backend/tests/test_guardian_state.py
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_guardian_feedback.py tests/test_guardian_state.py tests/test_delivery.py tests/test_eval_harness.py::test_main_lists_available_scenarios tests/test_eval_harness.py::test_runtime_eval_scenarios_expose_expected_details tests/test_eval_harness.py::test_guardian_judgment_runtime_eval_exposes_conflict_suppression -q
- cd docs && npm run build && git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(guardian): deepen world-model judgment

Summary:
- add contradiction-aware guardian world-model confidence with focus provenance and judgment-risk grounding
- resolve live guardian learning across global, thread, project, and thread-plus-project scopes before policy-time delivery and state synthesis
- record the Batch J slice review trail and shipped behavior in the implementation docs

Rationale:
- Batch J needed stronger world-model caution when live observer context conflicts with durable recall or weakly corroborated focus
- the previous global-only live learning window could hide the project- or thread-scoped outcome history that should actually drive interruption behavior
- the implementation docs needed to match the real guardian-state and delivery behavior now on this branch

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/guardian/world_model.py backend/src/guardian/state.py backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/src/guardian/learning_evidence.py backend/src/guardian/learning_arbitration.py backend/src/guardian/feedback.py backend/src/observer/delivery.py backend/tests/test_guardian_feedback.py backend/tests/test_guardian_state.py backend/tests/test_delivery.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py backend/tests/test_strategist_tick.py
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_guardian_feedback.py tests/test_guardian_state.py tests/test_delivery.py tests/test_eval_harness.py::test_main_lists_available_scenarios tests/test_eval_harness.py::test_runtime_eval_scenarios_expose_expected_details tests/test_eval_harness.py::test_guardian_judgment_runtime_eval_exposes_conflict_suppression -q
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(api): decouple descriptions from live snapshots

Summary:
- build EtherCAT.describe/1 and EtherCAT.inventory/0 from master-held
  configured slave summaries instead of live slave snapshots
- carry the driver config and alias map through configured slave status so
  effective endpoint descriptions can be rebuilt without snapshot reads
- tighten docs and tests around the split between configuration-backed
  interface metadata and live snapshot values

Rationale:
- slave descriptions should answer what the configured interface is, not
  depend on current snapshot availability
- snapshots remain the right place for current endpoint values, cycle tags,
  and other live state
- separating these contracts makes the public API easier to reason about and
  matches the intended Ogol integration boundary more closely

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test
- mix test test/ethercat/bus_test.exs:795
- mix test test/ethercat/bus_test.exs:909
- ../ogol: mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Archive historical docs and add documentation index

Reorganize the docs directory so current operational guidance stays easy to find while historical notes remain available for project archaeology.

This keeps the active deployment note at docs/HOWTO_SYSTEMD.md, adds lightweight index files for docs/ and docs/history/, and moves legacy implementation reports, assistant handoff notes, bugfix summaries, and planning documents into the history archive.

CLAUDE.md now points explicitly at the new documentation structure so future work uses the current source of truth instead of stale implementation notes.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: mark
repository.description: Markdown editor. Web-based, works directly with your local files, no registration or web storage required; optionally, it can also work with .md files from your GitHub account.

commit.message:
Refactor app shell and remove stale runtime integrations

Consolidate the cleanup pass that removed legacy analytics, dead theme plumbing, and several runtime leftovers from earlier architecture iterations.

This drops the WebAnalytics component and related documentation to align the product with the zero-data-collection promise, removes unused initialization and theme helpers, simplifies settings and toolbar props, and fixes the tab/preview refactor regressions that left the editor blank.

It also hardens the editor theme fallback path, localizes bundled fonts and syntax-highlighting styles, strips old CDN/importmap references from the app shell and service worker, updates PWA asset references, and brings markdown help/lint/format behavior back into sync.

Build verification was run through the production systemd path after the cleanup to ensure the deployed Vite build succeeds with the new asset graph.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(api): add endpoint descriptions and slave aliases

Summary:
- add public endpoint and slave description structs plus EtherCAT.inventory/0
- let slave configs alias driver-native endpoint names per slave
- expose alias-applied endpoint names through snapshots, descriptions,
  events, and generic :set_output command handling
- update built-in IO drivers, tests, and README to the endpoint-first
  contract

Rationale:
- describe/1 was too thin to be a real machine-facing contract for Ogol
- drivers should describe native hardware while slave config owns
  machine-facing naming
- keeping the runtime slave-centric while making endpoint metadata explicit
  simplifies app binding without inventing a second runtime model

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix docs
- mix test
- ../ogol: mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(reach): stabilize runtime reach eval mocks

Summary:
- normalize non-scalar websocket and daemon runtime inputs at the channel-routing helper boundary
- pin explicit websocket active-count fixtures in reach and delivery eval scenarios that model a live browser path
- add a regression test for non-scalar runtime state and record the CI follow-up in the Presence and Reach implementation log

Rationale:
- the new runtime reach-health contract assumed strict scalar transport state, but several eval scenarios and mocks still used bare MagicMock values
- that contract drift caused CI failures in eval coverage once runtime route inspection started reading websocket and daemon reachability everywhere
- fixing the helper boundary and the affected eval fixtures restores deterministic behavior without loosening the reach-health model

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/extensions/channel_routing.py backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/tests/test_extensions_api.py
- git diff --check
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_extensions_api.py::test_channel_routing_defaults_surface_active_adapters tests/test_extensions_api.py::test_channel_routing_runtime_status_tolerates_non_scalar_mock_state -q
- cd docs && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* refactor: replace provider-failover recipe with circuit-breaker

Failover between providers is a client-side pattern, not a Fairvisor
feature. Replace with a focused circuit-breaker recipe that demonstrates
the actual cost-spike auto-shutdown capability:
- spend_rate_threshold_per_minute triggers full traffic block
- auto_reset_after_minutes provides hands-free cooldown
- per-org TPM limit continues to run in parallel

* docs: add wrapper mode to README + integration links in comparison section

Refs #17 #19 from job discussion

* docs: rewrite LLM token budget section to showcase wrapper mode

* docs: wrapper mode selector pathPrefix "/" covers all providers

* docs: replace ASCII architecture diagrams with Mermaid sequence diagrams

* docs: fix JWT wording — Fairvisor parses claims, does not validate signature

* trim README: remove benchmark methodology, Contributing section; fix recipe name

* README: rework tagline, add hook + mode selector, drop Policy as code + ngx.shared.dict refs

* README: remove hero latency/RPS line

* README: broaden hook paragraph — not LLM-only

* docs: add 'Why we built this' section and ToC entry

* fix(recipes): replace unsupported loop_detector rule with spec-level loop_detection config

* fix(recipes): change cost_based period "30d" → "7d" (only 5m/1h/1d/7d are valid)

* docs: rewrite 'no external state' bullet to focus on request latency

* docs: reword 'does not replace' — Fairvisor can run standalone or alongside existing gateways

* fix(nginx): increase map hash size for quickstart

* fix(nginx): set map_hash_max_size 131072 to cover all 85k ASN entries

* fix(nginx): map_hash_max_size 262144 — need ~3x entries for collision-free hash (85k ASNs)

* fix(cli): add bin/fairvisor-cli with corrected -I path for cli.* modules

* fix(cli): Dockerfile.cli → bin/fairvisor-cli, ENTRYPOINT updated

* docs(cli): rename fairvisor → fairvisor-cli, fix resty -I path in docs

* docs(readme): fairvisor → fairvisor-cli in CLI section

* feat(limiter): improve token count accuracy and max_completion_tokens support

- Fix _simple_word_estimate to properly skip false-positive "content" key
  matches (e.g. when key appears inside a string value) by verifying
  the value separator (: "...") before counting characters
- Add _extract_max_tokens() to parse max_tokens / max_completion_tokens
  from raw request body when request_context.max_tokens is not set,
  enabling accurate budget reservation for OpenAI-compatible payloads

Add two scenarios to exercise the uncovered branches in _extract_max_tokens:
- body contains "max_tokens" field → uses it (covers return tonumber(val) branch)
- body has no max_tokens/max_completion_tokens → falls back to default_max_completion
  (covers return nil branch)

Fixes coverage regression (91.61% < 91.63% threshold).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace "Turn API limits into enforceable business policy." with

more direct, audience-specific, and immediately recognisable for
the developer reading on GitHub.

no -cli suffix). Renames bin/fairvisor-cli → bin/fairvisor, updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: remove fairvisor connect from CLI section (SaaS not yet available)

* fix(cli): handle nil read_err in validate command when file read returns nil

* fix(cli): fix nil read_err crash in test command; add lua-resty-http to CLI image

* fix(cli): remove version pin from lua-resty-http opm install (wrong version caused build failure)

* fix(supply-chain): pin lua-resty-http=0.17.1 and fix OPM version checker

- Pin ledgetech/lua-resty-http to 0.17.1 in Dockerfile.cli (0.17.2
  was never successfully published to OPM; 0.17.1 is the latest
  working release)
- Fix cross-line regex bug in check_unpinned_dependencies.py: the
  optional version group used \s+ which matched across newlines,
  causing the next-line token (e.g. WORKDIR) to be captured as the
  "version" — silently masking missing pins
- Add '=' in package check so opm get pkg=version syntax is correctly
  recognised as pinned (OPM embeds the version in the package arg, not
  as a separate space-separated token)
- Add supply_chain_pins_spec test asserting the CLI Dockerfile pins
  lua-resty-http to 0.17.1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): move mock_ngx to cli/lib so fairvisor test works in Docker

spec/helpers/mock_ngx.lua was not copied into the CLI Docker image
(Dockerfile.cli only copies src/, cli/, bin/fairvisor), causing
'module spec.helpers.mock_ngx not found' at runtime.

Move the helper to cli/lib/mock_ngx.lua where it belongs for CLI use,
and update the require path in cli/commands/test.lua.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): status command reads policy_version from /readyz; show 0 decisions on fresh edge

Policy version was always "unknown" because the code looked for a
fairvisor_bundle_version Prometheus metric that doesn't exist. The
version is in the /readyz JSON body as policy_version — parse it there.

Decisions was "unknown" on a fresh edge because Prometheus counters
are not emitted before the first increment. Show "0" instead of
"unknown" when the counter is absent from /metrics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(lint): exclude cli/lib/mock_ngx.lua from luacheck

mock_ngx.lua is a test-infrastructure helper copied from
spec/helpers/mock_ngx.lua (which was already excluded). The file
contains intentional patterns that predate luacheck adoption.
Exclude the cli/lib copy to restore green lua-quality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rule_engine): log state transitions at INFO instead of WARN

* docs: fix decision service allow status in README

commit.message:
Tighten split+UE verify gating for no-core rehearsals

The public split plus UE repo-local lane was failing verify when the
UE reached startup and the simulation evidence stayed green, but the
runtime logs never emitted the oaitun token because the lane has no AMF
or real core behind it. The runtime verify gate now keeps UE startup as
required, drops the tunnel-configured requirement for simulation-only
core-simulated lanes, and exercises that no-AMF path in the public
example regression while leaving the generic UE runtime check intact.

Constraint: The repo-local split plus UE lane must remain simulation-only and must not imply live-core proof
Rejected: Force ue_log_tun_configured to pass in the no-AMF lane | would misrepresent simulated-core behavior as a real-core success signal
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep ue_log_tun_configured as a verify gate only for lanes that can reach a real core; use observe metrics for simulation-only tunnel visibility
Tested: MIX_ENV=test mix test apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/cli_test.exs --only runtime_contract
Tested: mix compile --warnings-as-errors
Tested: MIX_ENV=test mix run -e '<custom command_runner verify repro for examples/ranctl/verify-oai-du-ue-repo-local.json>'
Not-tested: Real Docker/OAI repo-local runtime against live containers

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(reach): harden cross-surface delivery continuity

Summary:
- expose live route runtime health and selected fallback details in the observer and extensions APIs
- preserve same-thread continuation through native queued bundles and surface it in cockpit and daemon settings
- add reach-hardening regression coverage and update implementation docs for the shipped behavior

Rationale:
- make degraded browser, native, and packaged delivery legible from actual runtime state instead of static config
- keep continuation semantics stable when runtime reach changes or queued bundles share a live thread
- close review findings around mixed-session bundle collapse, stale queued-thread recovery, and hidden route states

Tests:
- git diff --check
- cd docs && npm run build
- cd frontend && NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-require-module npx vitest run src/components/settings/DaemonStatus.test.tsx src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_delivery.py -k "falls_back_to_native_when_browser_runtime_is_unavailable or preserves_shared_thread_continuity or deliver_queued_bundle_formats_correctly or no_active_channel_adapters_retains_queue or partitions_mixed_sessions_into_separate_notifications" -q
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_observer_api.py::TestObserverAPI::test_observer_continuity_snapshot tests/test_observer_api.py::TestObserverAPI::test_observer_continuity_recovers_queued_thread_from_intervention_outside_recent_window tests/test_extensions_api.py::test_channel_routing_defaults_surface_active_adapters -q
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -c "import asyncio, json; from src.evals.harness import run_runtime_evals; summary = asyncio.run(run_runtime_evals(['cross_surface_continuity_behavior'])); print(json.dumps({'failed': summary.failed, 'details': summary.results[0].details}, indent=2))"

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(codex): add cli hooks and empty-tail fallback

Summary:
- install Codex CLI Stop hooks and enable the codex_hooks feature flag
- add doctor and teardown coverage for Codex hook plus watch coexistence
- reproduce the VS Code Codex App empty-tail transcript shape in BDD
- prefer the newest non-empty Codex turn when watch last-turn-only
  sees an empty tail turn

Rationale:
- Codex CLI sessions were not reported because install only configured
  watch mode
- Codex App watch could select a trailing continuation turn with no
  assistant output
- a fixture derived from a real VS Code transcript keeps the regression
  test close to the observed failure shape

Tests:
- npm run --workspace @bububuger/spanory build

commit.message:
fix(trust): harden privileged capability repair paths

Summary:
- limit capability bootstrap autorepair to low-risk local toggles and keep higher-risk repair steps manual
- require shared lifecycle approval prechecks for starter-pack catalog installs and gate generated multi-step privileged repair bundles in the cockpit
- align backend/frontend/docs coverage with the actual risk-based approval contract and repair execution boundary

Rationale:
- broader workflow leverage had left planning and doctor-plan surfaces too close to privileged mutation paths
- starter-pack activation could still expand MCP-backed capability reach through catalog installs without the same approval seam as direct catalog installs
- generated repair bundles could still chain multiple privileged mutations from one click even after backend bootstrap hardening

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/capabilities.py backend/src/api/catalog.py backend/tests/test_capabilities_api.py backend/tests/test_catalog_api.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_capabilities_api.py -q
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_catalog_api.py -q
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_eval_harness.py::test_runtime_eval_scenarios_expose_expected_details tests/test_eval_harness.py::test_run_runtime_evals_passes_all_scenarios -q
- cd frontend && NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-require-module npm test -- --run src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- cd frontend && npm run build
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(cli): remove unpublished workspace deps from npm package

Summary:
- move private workspace packages out of install-time dependencies
- make public declaration files self-contained with local capture
  types
- add a packaged-manifest regression test for npm pack output

Rationale:
- published spanory tarballs were requiring private workspace
  packages that are not on npm, causing global installs and
  upgrade flows to fail
- the public d.ts files also referenced @bububuger/core types
  directly, so removing runtime deps required in-package type
  definitions

  test/bdd/package.manifest.integration.spec.ts

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Preserve the request-log metadata cleanup and stream collector hardening from main, keep the typed admin contract changes on top, and retain the debug-build metrics snapshot path needed for merge-target linting.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Implement live admin API key management

Addresses #26 and #31 by moving the admin API Keys surface to live gateway-backed create, list, and revoke flows, extending end-to-end contract coverage, and updating the control-plane documentation and ADR set.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix issue label deletion with Actions tokens (#37013)

Use shared repo permission resolution for Actions task users in issue
label remove and clear paths, and add a regression test for deleting
issue labels with a Gitea Actions token.

This fixes issue label deletion when the request is authenticated with a
Gitea Actions token.
Fixes #37011 

The bug was that the delete path re-resolved repository permissions
using the normal user permission helper, which does not handle Actions
task users. As a result, `DELETE
/api/v1/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{index}/labels/{id}` could return
`500` for Actions tokens even though label listing and label addition
worked.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: wxiaoguang <wxiaoguang@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Lunny Xiao <xiaolunwen@gmail.com>

commit.message:
Fix Docker bootstrap recovery on macOS (#73)

* fix(setup): preserve host workflow path in docker env

Summary:
- preserve the existing host WORKFLOW_FILE value when Docker setup runs
  against the /config-state mount
- avoid rewriting docker.env with the container-only
  /config-state/WORKFLOW.md path
- cover the mount-path regression with a setup rendering test

Rationale:
- remote Docker setup writes docker.env from inside the container, but
  Docker Compose later consumes that file on the host
- persisting the container path as WORKFLOW_FILE causes Docker Desktop
  bind mount failures on macOS during subsequent compose runs

Tests:
- cargo fmt
- cargo test setup::tests::docker_env_preserves_host_workflow_file_when_setup_runs_in_config_state -- --nocapture
- cargo test setup::tests -- --nocapture
- cargo test
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(install): repair stale docker env mount paths

Summary:
- repair invalid WORKFLOW_FILE values in managed docker.env before any
  docker compose invocation
- repair container-only SEED_REPO_PATH values the same way so the
  managed host checkout stays mountable
- make remote reconfigure runs recover broken installs in one pass

Rationale:
- once docker.env persisted /config-state/WORKFLOW.md, compose failed
  before setup could run and heal the config
- the host installer must sanitize managed bind-mount sources before it
  launches any compose-based command on macOS

Tests:
- bash -n scripts/install-remote-docker.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(api): add servicing order initiate endpoint (#23)

Summary:
- add a small Node HTTP API for POST /ServicingOrder/Initiate
- validate customer context, supported request types, and required request
  detail fields before creating orders
- cover the new contract with API tests and ignore validation-only
  node_modules installs

Rationale:
- the repo had no existing servicing-order endpoint or API coverage for the
  customer portal flow
- creating Pending orders with customer-context checks establishes the manual
  workflow entry point needed by downstream API and portal tickets
- returning a customer-safe payload keeps the stored audit note internal while
  preserving workflow tracking state

Tests:
- node --test tests/servicing-order-api.test.js
- npm test
- manual curl walkthroughs for 201 and 400 responses

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.description: openai symphony bian service request portal

commit.message:
fix(gateway): finalize stream collector before success path

Finalize the stream response collector before deciding usage accounting and request outcome so truncated terminal SSE frames cannot be recorded as successful streams. Add a gateway integration test covering a valid usage event followed by an incomplete final frame.\n\nCo-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(setup): persist GitHub token for Docker bootstrap (#72)

Summary:
- seed the remote Docker bootstrap env from GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN,
  or gh auth token when available on the host
- prompt for a GitHub token during setup when no env-backed token is
  available and persist it into the generated env file
- document the GH_TOKEN fallback and remote bootstrap token flow in
  the setup and troubleshooting guides

Rationale:
- the containerized setup flow could write a broken docker.env with an
  empty tracker token and then fail doctor validation immediately
- Docker bootstrap needs a token acquisition path that works before the
  first container run and still persists the secret for later runs

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
- bash -n scripts/install-remote-docker.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: kairastra
repository.description: Align intent. Launch execution. Land the merge.

commit.message:
fix(setup): replace remote bootstrap files automatically (#71)

Summary:
- auto-overwrite remote Docker bootstrap workflow and env files during
  interactive setup so doctor validates generated config instead of the
  placeholder files
- clarify setup prompts for the managed repo, workspace root, seed repo,
  and canonical clone URL
- default the clone URL from the selected target repo and accept SSH
  GitHub URLs in repo parsing

Rationale:
- the remote Docker installer pre-creates bootstrap files and users can
  answer "no" to overwrite prompts, which leaves invalid placeholders
  in place and causes doctor to fail with missing_tracker_kind
- the setup prompts also made it easy to mix the Kairastra installer repo
  with the repo being managed, which would seed the wrong clone target
  into workspaces

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(setup): support configurable project status mappings (#70)

* feat(setup): support configurable project status mappings

Summary:
- add workflow config for claimable and write-side project status
  mappings
- inspect project statuses in setup and gate normalization behind
  explicit confirmation and safety checks
- update doctor, docs, and the spec for custom project status flows

Rationale:
- let Kairastra work with repositories whose GitHub Projects use
  non-canonical status names
- preserve existing behavior by default while avoiding unsafe rewrites
  of live projects during setup

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(review): address PR feedback on status mappings

Summary:
- preserve explicit empty claimable-state configuration and fix
  project status overview totals
- drive review handoff and blocked-issue suppression from workflow
  status config
- align canonical setup statuses and bootstrap helper field-name
  handling

Rationale:
- address the concrete PR review findings without changing the
  overall status-mapping design
- prevent blocked issues from re-entering the queue until an
  operator changes their tracker state

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test
- python3 -m py_compile scripts/bootstrap_github_project.py

commit.message:
